model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Miami (FL) is the safer pick because its full-season predictive profile is modestly but consistently better than Missouri’s in both KenPom and Torvik, with edges on both offense and defense. The Hurricanes also bring the stronger record and a steadier overall résumé, led by high-level transfer production in a major turnaround season. Missouri has a credible upset path if its offense gets rolling and this becomes a shot-making game, but Miami looks like the more complete team over 40 minutes.

reasoningStep.title: Miami’s stronger two-way profile wins a close 7/10 call
reasoningStep.summary: This projects as a competitive game, but Miami grades out better in the key predictive systems and appears to have the more reliable overall team quality entering the tournament.
evidence: KenPom: Miami (FL) No. 30 with +20.67 adjusted efficiency margin vs Missouri No. 52 at +15.36.
Torvik: Miami (FL) No. 35 vs Missouri No. 46.
Offense/defense split: Miami 121.4 AdjO and 100.7 AdjD in KenPom vs Missouri 119.5 AdjO and 104.1 AdjD; Torvik also shows Miami ahead on both ends.

Recent team context from bracket coverage highlighted Miami’s major season turnaround under Jai Lucas, powered by Malik Reneau and Tre Donaldson; no major availability issue surfaced strongly enough to overturn the metrics edge.
Underdog path considered: Missouri’s offense is close enough to Miami’s to make this dangerous if the Tigers win the perimeter scoring battle, so confidence is moderate rather than high.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Miami March Madness opponent is Missouri in 2026 NCAA bracket Skip to main content Home Heat Dolphins Marlins High School Fishing Hurricanes Gators Seminoles Owls Golf USA TODAY Sports SportsbookWire Miami basketball’s March Madness opponent is Missouri. See the bracket Dan Rorabaugh USA TODAY NETWORK - Florida Show Caption Hide Caption Who’s ready to put on the glass slipper this March? Two underdog teams that could make a Cinderella run this March Madness are Saint Louis and Miami (Ohio). (This story has been updated to add new information about the other teams in Miami's March Madness bracket.) The Miami men's basketball team will play Missouri in the first round of the 2026 NCAA tournament . The Hurricanes (25-8) were awarded the No. 7 seed in the West Region. Jai Lucas ' first March Madness opponent as head coach will be the Tigers (20-12) out of the SEC. Miami completed a stunning turnaround in 2026 after going 7-24 last season. They returned to the postseason behind first-team All-ACC forward Malik Reneau and second-team guard Tre Donaldson, both Florida natives who transferred in from Indiana and Michigan, respectively. UM finished third in the ACC regular-season standings and reached the semifinals of the ACC tournament . Buy Miami NCAA tournament tickets The Hurricanes' biggest win of the season came Feb. 10 against North Carolina, when they beat Caleb Wilson and the Tar Heels 75-66 at the Watsco Center. They last appeared in the NCAA tournament in 2023, when they reached the Final Four as a 5-seed before losing to eventful champion UConn. Who does Miami play in 2026 NCAA tournament? Miami faces the Missouri Tigers in the first round. Mizzou went 20-12 and finished seventh in the SEC this season, though they were bounced by Kentucky in their SEC tournament opener in the quarterfinals. Missouri is in the big dance for the third time in four years under coach Dennis Gates. The Tigers boasted the No. 5 defense in their conference at 75.3 points per game and were 12th in offense at 79.7 ppg, though they were second in shooting 49% from the floor. Their best player is Mark Mitchell, a 6-foot-8 senior forward who made the All-SEC second team. He led the team in scoring with 18.3 ppg, good for sixth in the SEC, and in rebounding with 5.3 boards per game. Mizzou has two other players averaging double-digit points, Australian guard Jayden Stone and forward Trent Pierce. Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY's Bracket Challenge today! Bracket Challenge Sweepstakes | USA TODAY Sports When does Miami basketball play in March Madness 2026? Miami vs. Mizzou is set for Friday, March 20. Game time will be announced later. SURVIVOR POOL: Ready to win March Madness? Join USA TODAY's Survivor Pool today! Miami opponents in March Madness bracket If the Hurricanes advance to the second round, they will face either No. 2 Purdue or No. 15 Queens on Sunday, March 22. The Boilermakers (27-8) won the Big Ten tournament with guard Braden Smith and big man

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
